entities.name: orchestration engineering
entities.context: Technical strategy of coordinating many small models in sequence rather than relying on single high-end model prompts

entities.name: prompt engineering
entities.context: Contrasted approach of optimizing prompts for massive models, which V believes should be de-prioritized

entities.name: modularity
entities.context: Design principle that supports breaking complex tasks into discrete, narrow steps

entities.name: error correction
entities.context: Principle enabling inspection points where errors can be caught or diluted across steps

entities.name: task decomposition
entities.context: Breaking tasks into small, predictable steps to create multiple inspection points

entities.name: system resilience
entities.context: Quality that emerges from decomposition of tasks rather than single-shot approaches

entities.name: single points of failure
entities.context: Risk in complex systems when relying on high-end models that can hallucinate catastrophically

relationships.context: V believes orchestration engineering should replace prompt engineering as the primary technical strategy
Orchestration engineering is an application of the modularity principle
Orchestration engineering applies error correction by creating multiple inspection points
Decomposition into small steps produces system resilience and quality
Decomposition mitigates single points of failure like model hallucination
Orchestration engineering relies on decomposing tasks into discrete, narrow steps
